Solution(s): 1. Install a belt cleaner at the head pulley
2. Establish a new belt compound material for release; impression top

Cause(s): 1. Insufficient traction between belt and pulley
Solution(s): 1. Increase belt wrap with snub roller
2. Lag the drive pulley
3. Replace the pulley lagging

Problem: Belt runs to one side over long distance
Cause(s): 1. Off-center loading from material buildup in chutes
Solution(s): 1. Install a chute lining for wear resistance

Cause(s): 2. Idlers or pulleys are not square with center line of the belt
Solution(s): 2. Readjust idlers in the area affected
3. Make sure Pulleys are crowned

Problem: Belt breaks at or behind fasteners; fasteners pull out
Cause(s): 1. Fastener plates/legs too long for pulley size
Solution(s): 1. Replace with smaller fasteners; increase pulley size

Cause(s): 2. Wrong type of fastener; fasteners too tight or too loose
Solution(s): 2. Use proper fasteners and splice technique; set up schedule for fastener inspection

Problem: Belt splices separating / Finger popping up
Solution(s): 1. Reduce tension on belt. Belts should run at lowest tension possible without slippage
2. Remove material build up from pulleys and v-guide grooves
3. Install plow on pulley side of belt

Problem: Severe pulley cover (back of belt) wear
Solution(s): 1. Free idlers; improve maintenance and lubrication
2. Increase tension through screw take-up or add counterweight
3. Improve loading and transfer conditions; install cleaning devices; improve maintenance
4. Add lagging to drive pulley

Cause(s): 1. In most cases a foreign object is coming into contact with the cleats that are failing
Solution(s): 1. Inspect system for any obstructions that are coming into contact with cleats

Cause(s): 2. Product is too heavy for current cleats
Solution(s): 2. Add gussets or fabric reinforcement to cleats

Cause(s): 3. Jams in loading area causing cleats to “dig” material out
Solution(s): 3. More uniform loading of material on belt
1. Switch to a notched / Modified V-Guide profile
2. Track belt to run straight
3. Improve maintenance and clean all V-Guide grooves
1. Consult Accurate for best abrasive resistant material for your application
2. Use belt material rated for heat / oil

Cause(s): 3. Accumulation of material on top side of belt
Solution(s): 3. Try using friction top belt or add stop/start eye sensors
1. Switch to a belt material with a strength rating that will work for the loads on the belt
2. Less tension on take-ups
3. Add additional heat protection to belt

Cause(s): Spilled oil or grease, over lubrication of idlers
Solution(s): Improve housekeeping; reduce quantity of grease used; check grease seals
1. Replace with the proper belt
2. User larger-diameter pulleys
3. Use correct belt designed for specific conditions
